questions.q: Hampi was the capital of which great empire?
questions.explanation: Hampi was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire.

questions.q: On the banks of which river does Hampi stand?
questions.explanation: Hampi stands on the Tungabhadra river.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state are the ruins of Hampi located?
questions.explanation: Hampi lies in the modern state of Karnataka.

questions.q: The ruins of Hampi are recognised as what?
questions.explanation: The Group of Monuments at Hampi is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: Hampi's landscape is famously dotted with what striking natural feature?
questions.explanation: Hampi sits amid a dramatic landscape of giant granite boulders.

questions.q: Which famous temple at Hampi is still an active place of worship today?
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha temple at Hampi remains an active centre of worship.

questions.q: The deity Virupaksha, worshipped at Hampi's main temple, is a form of which god?
questions.explanation: Virupaksha is a form of the god Shiva, the patron deity of Hampi.

questions.q: Which empire's defeat in 1565 led to Hampi being abandoned as a ruin?
questions.explanation: After Vijayanagara's defeat at Talikota in 1565, Hampi was sacked and left in ruins.

questions.q: Hampi served as a great centre of which activity during the empire's height?
questions.explanation: At its height Hampi was a thriving centre of temple-building, trade, and culture.

questions.q: The Vittala temple complex at Hampi is one of its most famous what?
questions.explanation: The Vittala temple complex is among Hampi's most celebrated monuments.

questions.q: The Vittala temple at Hampi is celebrated for which extraordinary feature?
options: Musical stone pillars and an iconic stone chariot
A marble dome of inlaid gems
questions.explanation: The Vittala temple is famous for its resonant musical pillars and its stone chariot shrine.

questions.q: The famous stone chariot at Hampi stands in the courtyard of which temple?
questions.explanation: The iconic stone chariot is located in the Vittala temple complex.

questions.q: Hampi is associated with which place from the Ramayana, identified with the surrounding region?
questions.explanation: The Hampi area is traditionally identified with Kishkindha, the legendary monkey kingdom of the Ramayana.

questions.q: Which large monolithic statue at Hampi depicts a fierce man-lion form of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The colossal Ugra (Lakshmi) Narasimha monolith at Hampi depicts the man-lion avatar of Vishnu.

questions.q: The Lotus Mahal at Hampi is an example of what kind of architecture?
options: A blend of Hindu and Islamic (Indo-Islamic) styles
questions.explanation: The Lotus Mahal in the royal enclosure shows a characteristic fusion of Hindu and Islamic architectural elements.

questions.q: The large stepped tank and elephant stables at Hampi were part of which area of the city?
questions.explanation: The royal centre of Hampi held structures like the stepped tank, the elephant stables, and palace platforms.

questions.q: Foreign visitors such as the Portuguese described Hampi at its peak as a city comparable in size to which European city?
questions.explanation: Portuguese chronicler Domingo Paes described Hampi as a great city, comparing it in size to Rome.

questions.q: The towering gateway of the Virupaksha temple at Hampi is an example of which architectural element?
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha temple is entered through a tall, tiered gopuram, a hallmark of South Indian temple architecture.

questions.q: Hampi was richly enriched by markets that traded in which luxury goods near its temple streets?
questions.explanation: Travellers recorded bustling market streets at Hampi trading in gems, horses, spices, and fine cloth.

questions.q: The Hazara Rama temple at Hampi is notable for carved panels depicting which epic?
questions.explanation: The Hazara Rama temple's walls are covered with reliefs narrating scenes from the Ramayana.

questions.q: Roughly when did Hampi flourish as the imperial capital before its destruction?
questions.explanation: Hampi flourished from the empire's founding in 1336 until its sacking in 1565, spanning the 14th to 16th centuries CE.

questions.q: Hampi's well-engineered network of canals and tanks was mainly built to support what?
options: Irrigation and water supply for the city
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara's engineers built extensive aqueducts, canals, and tanks to supply water and irrigate the region around Hampi.

questions.q: The Krishna temple at Hampi was built by Krishnadevaraya to commemorate his conquest of which place?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya built the Krishna temple at Hampi to mark his victory and the capture of Udayagiri during his eastern campaigns.

questions.q: The royal platform at Hampi from which kings watched festivals and parades is known as the:
questions.explanation: The Mahanavami Dibba was a great stone platform from which Vijayanagara kings observed the grand Mahanavami (Dasara) festivities.

questions.q: The Tungabhadra at Hampi was historically crossed near the city using what traditional craft?
options: Pontoon rafts of inflated leather only
questions.explanation: Round wicker-and-hide coracles have long been used to cross the Tungabhadra at Hampi, a practice noted by early travellers.

questions.q: Which sacred hill overlooking Hampi is associated in legend with the birthplace of the monkey god Hanuman?
questions.explanation: Anjanadri hill near Hampi is traditionally revered as the birthplace of Hanuman.

questions.q: The grand festival celebrated with great pomp at Hampi, described by foreign visitors, was the nine-day:
questions.explanation: The nine-day Mahanavami festival was a spectacular state occasion at Hampi, vividly described by visitors like Abdur Razzaq and Domingo Paes.

questions.q: Which Persian ambassador left a detailed 15th-century account of Vijayanagara/Hampi's wealth and bazaars?
questions.explanation: The Persian envoy Abdur Razzaq visited in the 15th century and described Hampi's fortifications, bazaars, and riches.

questions.q: The Vittala temple's stone chariot at Hampi is modelled on the chariot shrine of which other famous temple's tradition?
options: A temple car (ratha) like the Sun temple at Konark
questions.explanation: The stone chariot represents a temple ratha (car) and echoes the chariot-shaped design tradition seen at temples such as Konark.

questions.q: The fortified sacred precinct around the Virupaksha temple at Hampi formed part of which historic centre of the city?
options: The Hampi (sacred) bazaar centre near the riverside
questions.explanation: The Virupaksha temple anchored the sacred centre of Hampi, fronted by the long colonnaded Hampi bazaar near the Tungabhadra.
